name: Candidate Selection and Fixture Planning Gate
decision: Select read-surface polish as the only Phase 20.2 candidate; do not select any Go write, file, migration, system, server, import/export, cleanup, or frontend ownership candidate.

selected_candidate.title: Tighten read-only parity before any write ownership

python: All POST / PUT / DELETE / PATCH routes
Files, uploads, attachments, cleanup, import/export, frontend/static assets, and migrations

selected_candidate.reason: It improves the already-promoted read-only surface without changing runtime ownership, Caddy matchers, SQLite query_only mode, frontend defaults, production DB write behavior, or exposure boundaries.

rejected_candidates.reason: High-risk DB and file side effects require transaction, Note_History, Source_Urls, tag/category, image cleanup, CSRF/origin, backup, and rollback parity before selection.
Bulk and action writes need partial-failure transaction fixtures and sort/tag duplication parity before selection.
Nonnumeric and nested /api/notes/... paths intentionally remain Python-owned under the hardened Caddy matcher.
Mutation behavior still needs uniqueness/collation, default-category, reassignment, and rollback fixtures before selection.
File write/delete and content restore behavior require manifest/checksum rollback planning before selection.
Upload validation, path safety, image metadata parsing, and SSRF guard parity are out of scope for a read-polish gate.
Cleanup routes are destructive file maintenance and require dry-run plus restore evidence before selection.
Export is data exfiltration surface and import is bulk write surface; neither should be mixed into read-surface polish.
System routes and migration status remain Python operational surface and schema SSOT.
Server routes are localhost-only operational endpoints with service/process and backup file side effects.
Configuration writes need file-locking and config backup behavior before selection.

fixture_plan.future_go_comparison_owner: go-shadow read-only runtime against the same temp_db copy

cases: notes list pagination and per_page bounds
document whether attachment metadata and text-file body search are Python-only gaps or Go parity requirements
No Go route expansion for nonnumeric or nested /api/notes/... paths
No Go routing header on Python-owned system/server routes
No Go ownership for write methods
Go source still contains no POST/PUT/DELETE route registration

rollback_plan.reason: 20.2 is documentation and test planning only; it performs no live Pi, Caddy, systemd, frontend, DB, or Go runtime change.
rollback_evidence_required_before_any_fu: Fresh timestamped SQLite backup before live routing or write experiments
Caddy config backup before any matcher edit
Proof that prism-go-readonly.service remains localhost-bound and query_only for read-only work
Header/status/log checks proving Python-owned routes stay Python-owned
Documented revert command or config restore path for any future runtime change

stop_conditions_for_20_3: Python vs Go response diff mismatch on the hardened read surface
Go search behavior cannot be made equivalent without file access beyond the current read-only contract
Any proposed change requires POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH, production DB writes, Go migrations, file writes/deletes, or Caddy route expansion
sqlite_query_only would need to be disabled
The Caddy matcher would need to broaden beyond exact read paths and numeric note detail
A route would become exposed outside localhost, trusted LAN, VPN, SSH tunnel, or an externally authenticated reverse proxy
Frontend default API target or Python rollback ownership would need to change
not_authorized_by_20_2: Caddy route expansion beyond the validated GET read surface
Changing prism-go-readonly.service away from SQLite query_only
Live Pi service or Caddy reload

result.conclusion: Phase 20.2 selects read-surface polish only. The next safe step is a separately approved 20.3 read-surface parity and documentation polish gate, not Go write implementation.

allowed_next_step.title: Read Surface Parity and Documentation Polish Gate
allowed_next_step.scope: Plan and execute only read-only parity fixture improvements and documentation alignment for the existing hardened Go read surface. It must not expand Caddy routes, disable query_only, add Go writes/files/migrations, change frontend defaults, remove Python, reload live services, or broaden public exposure.
not_authorized_without_approval: Caddy route expansion beyond the validated GET read surface
Changing prism-go-readonly.service away from SQLite query_only
Live Pi service or Caddy reload
